LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION AS A MERITLESS SLAPP PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. DEFENDANT ESTABLISHED THAT THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES CONDUCT GOVERNED BY ANTI-SLAPP. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF CAN PREVAIL ON ANY OF THE ALLEGED CAUSES OF ACTION. THE CONDUCT ALLEGED IS PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILIGE AND/OR WAS DECIDED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT AND AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN CASE NO. 619215. ORDER ON SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED ON APPEAL IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $9387.50 IN FEES. THE BILLING RATE AND HOURS EXPENDED ARE REASONABLE AND DEFENDANT IS THE PREVAILING PARTY. CCP SECTION 1032 (A) (4); CRC 8.278 (A)(1) AND (2); MOORE V. LIU (1999) 69 CAL. APP. 4TH 745, 754-755. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees Incurred On Appeal
Off Calendar Sep-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joanna Kozubal (864.6962) for defendant and moving party
Richard Canatella (584.5446) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES INCURRED ON APPEAL is granted. Defendant is awarded $9387.50 in fees. The billing rate and hours expended are reasonable and defendant is the prevailing party. CCP section 1032 (a) (4); CRC 8.278 (a)(1) and (2); Moore v. Liu (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 745, 754-755. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jan-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES.
APPEARANCES: RICHARD CANATELLA, ESQUIRE (COTTER & DEL CARLO, 415.584.5446) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOANNA KOZUBAL, ESQUIRE (415.864.6962) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS A RIGHT TO ATTORNEY'S FEES UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 425.16(C). ORDER AND AMENDED JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint;
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HENRY STEVENS' Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.
APPEARANCES: Richard A. Canatella, Esq. of Law Offices of Cotter & Del Carlo 584-5446 for pltf & moving party; Brenda Cruz Keith, Esq. 626-6494 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied based on the decision in Simmons vs. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001) 92 Cal. App. 4th 1068. Order on motion for leave to file first amended complaint signed in open court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike First, Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action As A Meritless Slapp Pursuant To Ccp 425.16;Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S Special Motion To Strike First, Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action As A Meritless Slapp Pursuant To CCP 425.16.
APPEARANCES: Richard A. Canatella, Esq. of Law Offices of Cotter & Del Carlo 584-5446 for pltf & opposing party; Brenda Cruz Keith, Esq. 626-6494 for deft & moving party.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Defendant established that the complaint alleges conduct governed by Anti-SLAPP. Plaintiff has failed to establish that plaintiff can prevail on any of the alleged causes of action. The conduct alleged is protected by the litigation privilige and/or was decided in favor of defendant and against plaintiff in case no. 619215. Order on special motion to strike signed in open court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
